conversations.content: Let's do some magic exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please explain using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday: As a Frost specialization mage, if I want to use "Grasp of Frost", what prerequisites must be met? What scenarios is it most suitable for?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday, casting rules for the frost magic "Grasp of Frost"</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:30] Time: 2026-08-19-10:15, Content: The user taught the casting rules for the frost magic "Frost Grasp" in Azeroth. The prerequisites are the Mage class (Frost specialization) and Revered reputation with the Kirin Tor; the core effect is summoning a frost palm to grasp all enemies in the target area, rooting them for 3 seconds and dealing moderate frost damage, with an additional area frost burst upon the root's expiration; applicable to arena crowd control and rooting groups of mobs in dungeons. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:30] gives the detailed casting rules for "Grasp of Frost". Information needs to be extracted from three dimensions: 1. Prerequisites: Mage class (Frost specialization), Exalted reputation with the Kirin Tor; 2. Core effect: summons a frost palm to grasp all enemies in the target area, roots them for 3 seconds and deals moderate frost damage, with an additional AoE frost burst at the end of the root; 3. Applicable scenarios: arena control, dungeon group rooting scenarios.
conversations.content: To learn and cast "Grasp of Frost", the following prerequisites must be met: Mage class (Frost specialization), Exalted reputation with the Kirin Tor. This spell is most suitable for arena control and dungeon group rooting scenarios.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
